summary: Set 101 Session 1 of 2: a fresh Build now declares the real `default` module (docs/modules.yaml) and scaffolds its two lifecycle sets via the Set 087/098 writers (scaffoldNewModule + scaffoldModuleLifecycleSets, reused verbatim, never modified) -- the Class1 starter, rename-or-delete one action away via the Set 099/100 Work Explorer actions. New scaffoldDefaultModuleAndLifecycleSets() + BuildStructureSeams.scaffoldDefaultModule test seam in gitScaffold.ts, called from buildProjectStructureNoPrompt right after the scaffold step. Gated on docs/modules.yaml having been freshly created by THIS Build call AND no pre-existing docs/session-sets/ content (listSessionSetDirNames), so a Build re-run and a legacy repo (with or without a manifest) are left byte-for-byte untouched -- the invariant lives on the scaffold function itself, not just the caller's gate. Added the Default-is-Class1 short paragraph to both consumer-bootstrap templates (getting-started / start-here), regenerated the cold-start golden fixtures, Python cold-start acceptance test passing both tiers. VERIFICATION STORY (6 rounds, 3 remediation cycles, 1 operator intervention): discovery round 1 (fan-out K=2, gpt-5-6) found 4 blocking Majors, all real -- (1&2, one bug) the original gate treated 'manifest just created' as proof of a fresh repo, so a legacy repo with pre-existing session sets but no manifest would have been wrongly seeded with Default on its first post-upgrade Build (fixed with the listSessionSetDirNames refusal + real-fs and wiring tests); (3) the dogfood bypassed the packaged/interactive layer; (4) no Work Explorer end-state tests. Supplementary pass: nothing new. Remediation round 1 (VSIX packaged via vsce + a fixture-seeded real-webview Playwright spec + tree-model tests) was accepted by remediation-review round 3, but the close_session BACKSTOP (round 4) re-raised the literal 'walk Default -> rename -> delete -> re-add against the locally built VSIX' demand. A neutral third-provider opinion (gemini-pro, s1-third-opinion-vsix-dispute.json) judged the literal demand infeasible but named a real narrower gap (command-registration dispatch untested); remediation round 2 added an offline stub-callback wiring test -- which remediation-review round 5 REJECTED as still not the real journey. The operator then directed continued work on the real walkthrough rather than adjudicating it away, and remediation round 3 DELIVERED IT: vsix-first-run-walkthrough.spec.ts drives a real VS Code Electron instance through the extension's actual UI end to end -- the real Build button in the real Getting Started webview (real git init, real venv + network pip install, the new default-module scaffold), then rename (QuickPick + two InputBoxes + modal confirm) -> delete (QuickPick + modal confirm) -> re-add (two InputBoxes), asserting the rendered tree after every step. The session's own earlier 'technically infeasible' judgment (seconded by the third opinion) was WRONG and the verifier was right to hold the line; the five real automation blockers are documented in the spec header and s1-remediation-round-3.md (activation-on-view-reveal; client-side budget validation silently no-ops Build; F1's command prefix wiped by fill(); QuickPick placeholders are attributes not text; window.dialogStyle defaults NATIVE on desktop -- fixed via --enable-smoke-test-driver, with an additive optional extraArgs parameter on launchVSCode). Remediation-review round 6: VERIFIED, 0 blocking, 0 minor findings; fix verdicts 1 accepted + 2 accepted-with-modification + 0 rejected; two explicitly low-risk nits recorded (the harness is --extensionDevelopmentPath over the identical compiled dist/extension.js the .vsix packages, plus vsce package succeeding, not a literal archive install -- the install-based @vscode/test-electron harness is documented broken on this machine; and gitScaffoldDefaultModule.test.ts's 'both tiers' tree test iterates tier labels without re-driving Build dispatch, which the fake-seam gating tests cover separately). The third-remediation-cycle continuation past the 2-cycle bound was operator-directed, satisfying the stop-to-operator rule. CANDIDATE LESSON for Step 9 / close-out consideration: an infeasibility claim about test automation requires an attempted spike, not an argument from documentation -- the repo's own 'brittle/broken' notes described a retired popup and a different harness, and the literal walkthrough took ~5 iterations once actually attempted. Suites green at close: extension unit 1618/1618 (baseline 1605, +13 net new); tsc --noEmit clean; eslint clean on all touched files (repo baseline unchanged: 7 pre-existing errors in untouched test files); esbuild clean; Playwright Layer 3 28/28 (26 baseline + 2 new specs, 0 regressions; L-064-12 satisfied); ai_router pytest 3030 passed/6 skipped (unchanged baseline, no Python touched). Transient infrastructure note: the verification loop stalled mid-session on a persistent OpenAI 429 (9 attempts; the pinned gpt-5-6 verifier's account was out of credit) -- work was committed before retrying per the provider-failure guidance, and the operator's credit top-up resolved it. No version bump, no publish -- the single release boundary for the module-lifecycle-simplification bundle (Sets 098-101) lands after Session 2 closes Set 101.

reason.specifics: Session 2 is primarily technical-prose authorship (the hello-world tutorial + quick-start rewrite, the new module-reorganization.md guidance doc) plus a repo-wide doc-consistency sweep and the standard close-out mechanics; the same orchestrator identity keeps continuity and strong prose quality, per the routed recommendation in s1-next-set-analysis.json.
